code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

from typing import List
def predict(triples: List[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language sentence from a list of Triple objects."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a rendering lambda (subject, object) -> phrase
    phrase_map = {
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "industry": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates in the {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is headquartered in {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a total area of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} holds the title {o}",
        "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
        "populationTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population of {o}",
        "netIncome": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s net income is {o}",
        "operatingIncome": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s operating income is {o}",
        "revenue": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s revenue is {o}",
        "numberOfEmployees": lambda s, o: f"{s} employs {o} people",
        "numberOfLocations": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} locations",
        "service": lambda s, o: f"{s} provides {o} services",
        "type": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} has key person {o}",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"The cost of {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "product": lambda s, o: f"{s} produces {o}",
        "subsidiary": lambda s, o: f"{s} has subsidiary {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "motto": lambda s, o: f"The motto of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} belongs to {o}",
        "areaUrban": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an urban area of {o}",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes {o}",
        "percentageOfAreaWater": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o}% water area",
        "longName": lambda s, o: f"The long name of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} is at {o} meters above sea level",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a government type of {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ethnic group {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to create a more natural single sentence
    from collections import defaultdict

    # subject -> predicate -> list of objects
    grouped = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        # format numbers with commas
        if obj.replace(".", "", 1).isdigit():
            try:
                num = float(obj)
                if num.is_integer():
                    obj = f"{int(num):,}"
                else:
                    obj = f"{num:,.2f}"
            except ValueError:
                pass
        grouped[t.subject][t.predicate].append(obj)

    # helper to render a predicate with its objects
    def render_predicate(pred, objs):
        # join multiple objects with commas and 'and'
        if len(objs) == 1:
            obj_str = objs[0]
        else:
            obj_str = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]

        render = phrase_map.get(pred)
        if render:
            # phrase_map expects (subject, object); we pass dummy subject for now
            # but we will prepend subject later, so use render with placeholder and strip subject later
            # Instead, call render with empty subject and then remove leading space if any
            phrase = render("", obj_str).strip()
            # remove leading verb if it starts with subject placeholder
            return phrase.lstrip()
        else:
            return f"{pred} {obj_str}"

    # Build clauses per subject
    subject_clauses = []
    for subj, pred_dict in grouped.items():
        parts = []
        for pred, objs in pred_dict.items():
            # Use the subject only once at the start of the clause
            phrase = render_predicate(pred, objs)
            parts.append(phrase)
        # Combine parts with commas and 'and' for the last part
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {parts[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]
        subject_clauses.append(clause)

    if not subject_clauses:
        return ""

    # Join different subject clauses with semicolons for readability, ending with a period
    final = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."
    return final

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
